text.title: Plan-Execute vs ReAct Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every agent-loop selection MUST enforce.

rule.statement: When known_substeps is 3-10 AND adaptability_needed == false AND horizon <= 10, the spec MUST select Plan-Execute.
rule.rationale: Plan-Execute auditability + parallelisability beat ReAct adaptability when steps are predictable.

rule.statement: When known_substeps == 'unknown' OR adaptability_needed == true, the spec MUST select ReAct.
rule.rationale: Each Action's Observation reshapes the next Thought; only ReAct adapts to surprising tool outputs.

rule.statement: When horizon > 10 AND mixed_predictability == true, the spec MUST select Hybrid (top-level plan + per-step ReAct with max_turns=5).
rule.rationale: Top-level plan stays auditable; per-step loops bound exploration. The deep-agents pattern.

rule.statement: Every ReAct or Hybrid spec MUST set max_turns >= 1. Unbounded loops are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Infinite loops on bad tool output drain budget silently. max_turns is the only honest safeguard.

rule.statement: Plan-Execute MUST emit the plan in ONE call, then deterministic code dispatches execution. Plans embedded inside execution turns are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Mid-run plan mutation produces unauditable traces that no human can review.

rule.statement: Hybrid runs MUST aggregate per-step results into a single state document (<2K tokens). Re-loading full sub-results into the planner context is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Re-loading explodes context budget; slim state doc keeps the planner under 5K tokens of history.
